name: Crepes a La Neige (Crepes with Snow)
recipeInstructions:
Throughout Acadia, it was customary to make crepes at Candlemas, hiding objects such as medals, wedding rings, buttons, or pennies inside and using them to predict the future for those who found them. Since eggs were often scarce during this time, Acadians would substitute with spectacular results; hence some still refer to a snowfall as "being enough to make crepes with."
1. Mix all ingredients together to form a smooth dough.
2. Drop spoonfuls of batter into hot fat.
3. Fry for 2-3 minutes on each side.
4. Serve with molasses or grated maple sugar.
